dataset: Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive - Real drug interaction database

pi_items.source: Task scenario description - role of agent

pi_items.verified_value: Ibrutinib (Imbruvica) - Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor approved for chronic lymphocytic leukaemia
pi_items.source: FDA approval records and multiple clinical trials (NEJM, Nature Blood Cancer Journal)

pi_items.verified_value: Acenocoumarol - anticoagulant prescribed for rate-controlled atrial fibrillation
pi_items.source: Drug database cross-reference; clinically used anticoagulant

pi_items.verified_value: Fluconazole - moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor antifungal, causes ~3-fold increase in ibrutinib AUC
pi_items.source: Web search: FDA labeling, RxList, multiple drug interaction databases

pi_items.verified_value: Ketoconazole - strong CYP3A4 inhibitor antifungal, causes 24-29 fold increase in ibrutinib AUC
pi_items.source: Web search: NEJM, PMC (NCBIArticles), FDA ClinPharmR document, ScienceDirect

pi_items.verified_value: Ketoconazole - strong CYP3A4 inhibitor with 24-29x AUC increase vs fluconazole (3x) and acenocoumarol (no significant pharmacokinetic interaction)
pi_items.source: Web search: CYP3A4 drug-drug interaction literature; FDA drug interaction study papers

pi_items.verified_value: Ibrutinib is metabolized primarily (96% of microsomal clearance) by CYP3A4/5. Strong CYP3A4 inhibitors like ketoconazole prevent ibrutinib metabolism, dramatically increasing plasma exposure and toxicity risk.
pi_items.source: Web search: Multiple PMC articles, PubMed, NEJM, accessdata.fda.gov ClinPharmR document

pi_items.metadata_value: CLL patient on ibrutinib with polypharmacy requiring interaction screening
pi_items.verified_value: CLL (Chronic Lymphocytic Leukaemia) is an FDA-approved indication for ibrutinib for first-line and relapsed/refractory treatment. Patient with multiple co-medications requires mandatory drug-drug interaction screening before ibrutinib initiation.
pi_items.source: Web search: FDA approval announcements, NEJM, ASH journals, official IMBRUVICA.com indication page

pi_items.verified_value: Real Android application developed by Radboudumc (Nijmegen) and University of Liverpool Drug Interactions Group, uses real clinical drug interaction data
pi_items.source: Web search: cancer-druginteractions.org, Google Play Store, App Store listings

pi_items.verified_value: Do Not Coadminister (Red) - expected based on clinical guidelines and Liverpool traffic-light system classification for strong CYP3A4 inhibitor interactions
Ketoconazole is a strong (potent) CYP3A4 inhibitor causing 24-29 fold increase in ibrutinib Cmax and AUC
pi_items.source: Web search: Multiple peer-reviewed sources confirming strength of CYP3A4 inhibition

pi_items.verified_value: Fluconazole is a moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor causing ~3-fold increase in ibrutinib AUC
pi_items.source: Web search: FDA labeling, drug interaction databases (Drugs.com, RxList, Medscape)

pi_items.verified_value: Acenocoumarol is not a CYP3A4 inhibitor; interaction risk is primarily due to bleeding complications from combined anticoagulant + ibrutinib effect, not pharmacokinetic
pi_items.source: Web search: Clinical studies on ibrutinib + anticoagulant combinations; PMID 11406743

privileged_info_summary: Ibrutinib is an FDA-approved Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor for chronic lymphocytic leukaemia, metabolized primarily by CYP3A4. Three co-medications are screened: ketoconazole (strong CYP3A4 inhibitor causing 24-29x AUC increase, rated 'Do Not Coadminister'/Red), fluconazole (moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor causing ~3x AUC increase), and acenocoumarol (anticoagulant with no significant pharmacokinetic interaction). The Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive application contains real clinical drug interaction data from the University of Liverpool Drug Interactions Group.
